Off Calendar Oct-02-2024 Continued To Dec-11-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ENLIGHTENED MANAGEMENT COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Enlightened Entertainment Co.'s "motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint" is denied. Punitive damages are adequately alleged for the pleading stage. For example, EMC failed to pay plaintiff his lawful wages, so he complained of the illegality to the California Labor Commission. (Cmplt. 3:3-23.) Plaintiff then settled with EMC for half of what he was owed only to be fired the next day without cause. (Id.) Joint employer is also adequately alleged under California's liberal pleading standards. The sole contrary opinion EMC's memorandum cites is from a federal court that does not follow California procedural law. (Memo. 7:14-17.) Interest is an issue typically decided at trial after all facts are in. Medical expenses are not limited to workers' compensation. The motion to strike is not "a procedural 'line item veto' for the civil defendant." (PH II, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.5, which requires a declaration stating the "means" of meet-and-confer (in person, telephone or video conference). The new response date is October 30.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
